# QMan Library - LLM Index

## Overview
QMan (Queue Manager) is an ultra-lightweight cooperative task queue manager for Arduino and AVR-based microcontrollers. It allows writing non-blocking, asynchronous-style code using simple DSL macros without the overhead of a full RTOS.

**Version:** 1.0.2-beta (from QMan.h)
**License:** MIT
**Platforms:** AVR, STM32, ESP8266, ESP32 (any Arduino-compatible)

## Core Macros (from QMan.h)

| Macro | What it does |
|-------|---------------|
| `QMAN_TASK(name)` | Define a new task function |
| `QMAN_INIT` | Code that runs once when task starts |
| `QMAN_LOOP` | Infinite loop that repeats the task |
| `QMAN_SLEEP(duration)` | Pause task (duration uses _ms, _us, or _tick literals) |
| `QMAN_DUTY(duration)` | Fixed-frequency execution (auto-compensates for task time) |
| `QMAN_STOP` | Stop and remove task from queue |
| `QMAN_GO(task)` | Start task immediately |
| `QMAN_GO(task, delay)` | Start task after delay |
| `QMAN_TICK()` | Call in loop() to drive scheduler |
| `QMAN_TICK(delta)` | Manual tick with delta time |

## Time Literals (from QMan.h)

'''
cpp
QMAN_SLEEP(1000_ms);   // 1000 milliseconds
QMAN_SLEEP(500_us);    // 500 microseconds
QMAN_SLEEP(10_tick);   // 10 internal ticks
QMAN_DUTY(1000_ms);    // Run every 1 second exactly
'''

## Quick Start Example

'''
cpp
#include <QMan.h>

QMAN_TASK(blink) {
    QMAN_INIT { pinMode(LED_BUILTIN, OUTPUT); }
    QMAN_LOOP {
        digitalWrite(LED_BUILTIN, HIGH);
        QMAN_SLEEP(500_ms);
        digitalWrite(LED_BUILTIN, LOW);
        QMAN_SLEEP(500_ms);
    }
}

void setup() { QMAN_GO(blink); }
void loop() { QMAN_TICK(); }
'''

## Configuration

Change maximum number of tasks (default 16) BEFORE including QMan.h:

'''
cpp
#define QMAN_POOL_SIZE 32
#include <QMan.h>
'''

Change tick accuracy (default 32 microseconds):

'''
cpp
#define QMAN_TICK_MAX_US 16  // Smaller = more accurate, more CPU
#include <QMan.h>
'''

## Error Handling

'''
cpp
void onWarning(uint8_t code) {
    Serial.print("Warning: ");
    Serial.println(code);
}

void onError(uint8_t code) {
    Serial.print("Error: ");
    Serial.println(code);
}

void setup() {
    qman.OnWarning(onWarning);
    qman.OnError(onError);
}
'''

### Error Codes (enum QManError)

| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| 1 | `QMAN_ERR_POOL_OVERFLOW` | Dynamic queue full. System may reboot. |
| 2 | `QMAN_WARN_STATIC_LIMIT` | More tasks defined than POOL_SIZE |
| 3 | `QMAN_WARN_TICK_LIMIT` | Requested tick accuracy not possible |
| 4 | `QMAN_WARN_DUTY_LATE` | Task execution exceeded duty period |

## SLEEP vs DUTY

**SLEEP** (relative timing):
- Waits fixed duration AFTER task work completes
- Total period = work_time + sleep_time
- DRIFTS over time

**DUTY** (absolute timing):
- Maintains exact period regardless of work time
- Automatically reduces wait time if task took longer
- NO DRIFT

Example from QMan.h comments:

'''
cpp
QMAN_TASK(slowTask) {
    QMAN_LOOP {
        doWork();              // Takes 100ms
        QMAN_SLEEP(1000_ms);   // Total = 1100ms (drifts)
    }
}

QMAN_TASK(preciseTask) {
    QMAN_LOOP {
        doWork();              // Takes 100ms
        QMAN_DUTY(1000_ms);    // Total = 1000ms (exact)
    }
}
'''

## Internal Functions (from QMan.h)

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `qman.Setup()` | Sync internal clock with hardware (call in setup) |
| `qman.Now()` | Get current internal time in ticks |
| `qman.Started()` | Get when current task started |
| `qman.Len()` | Get number of tasks in queue |
| `qman.Clear()` | Remove all tasks and reset time |
| `qman.Sync(delta)` | Manually add delta ticks |

## Internal Types (from QMan.h)

'''
cpp
struct QTime {
    uint32_t ticks;  // Internal representation
};

struct Task {
    TaskFunc func;    // Function pointer to task
    uint32_t nextRun; // When to run (in ticks)
};
'''

## Key Internal Concepts

- **Zero-stack coroutines:** Uses `static void* __resumeAddr` and goto for state saving
- **Priority queue:** Tasks sorted by `nextRun` (most urgent at pool[count-1])
- **Interrupt safety:** `QManGuard` RAII class disables interrupts during queue operations
- **Time rollover safe:** Uses 32-bit unsigned arithmetic with signed difference comparison

## Related Files in Repository

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/QMan.h` | Main header (macros, class definition, literals) |
| `src/QMan.cpp` | Global instance `qman` and task counter |
| `examples/AsyncLCD.ino` | Non-blocking LCD output demo |
| `examples/SleepVsDuty.ino` | SLEEP vs DUTY comparison |
| `examples/TestStability.ino` | Timing accuracy test |
| `library.properties` | Arduino IDE metadata |
| `README.md` | Full documentation |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Version history |
